compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Implemented: rewrote plan_review_T5c.md with an explicit trust-boundary scope gate generalized across all four dimensions (authn/authz, encryption-in-transit, least-privilege, secret-lifecycle); folded in the plan-review advisories — mixed-scope rule (sub-checks apply only to boundary-crossing components), ambiguous-reachability fallback (silent-on-network => not-applicable), and a concrete 'lower severity = advisory' zero-trust caveat. Mirrored the framing in the T5c routing checklist (new leading trust_boundary key). T10 left unchanged; added test_t10_not_reframed_by_trust_boundary_generalisation. ADR 0018 records the decision + T10 exclusion. Live old-vs-new calibration (docs/calibration/T5c_trust_boundary.md): d251 positive stays fire; 8a1c false-positive flips fire->no-fire; 4702 stays no-fire. validate-routing + plan-review unit tests + make lint green.

compiled_state.description:
# Elevate trust-boundary crossing to an explicit framing in the T5c plan-review security overlay

## Context
Follow-up to a278 (container/leaf scrutiny + T10 endpoint-auth). Research on how mature
security / LLM-review projects avoid false positives converged on **trust-boundary crossing** as
the scope gate: a security concern is in scope only when a component is reachable by a LOWER-trust
actor (STRIDE Spoofing↔authn; OWASP ASVS 1.4.1 enforcement points; Semgrep/Anthropic
exploitability-over-category). Today T5c uses this idea only implicitly. Making it an explicit,
first-class framing (a) generalizes beyond auth to T5c's other dimensions and (b) sharpens the
reachability judgement — while keeping rebar's own local/in-process work false-positive-free.
Scope note: this is for **T5c only** — T10's `endpoint_access_contract` stays focused on infra
contract-completeness and must NOT be re-scoped to the general framing (approved: don't blur T10).

Files: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5c.md` (rubric), `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (T5c checklist if needed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plan_review_T5c.md` leads with an explicit TRUST-BOUNDARY scope gate: a security finding is in
      scope only when the plan introduces/exposes a component reachable by a lower-trust actor
      (public internet, another tenant, an untrusted network, an unauthenticated user); in-process /
      local-CLI / loopback-only surfaces PASS as not-applicable.
- [ ] The framing GENERALIZES beyond auth: the same gate governs T5c's existing dimensions
      (authn/authz, encryption-in-transit, least-privilege, secret lifecycle) — each fires only where
      a boundary is actually crossed.
- [ ] Preserves the positive-pass carve-out (pure library / in-process module / single-user local CLI /
      loopback-only → PASS, no auth demanded) so rebar's own local work stays FP-free.
- [ ] Zero-trust caveat encoded: a single-tenant / private network is LOWER severity, not exempt.
- [ ] T10 `endpoint_access_contract` is left unchanged (no blurring); a test/asserts T10 still fires
      only on infra intent.
- [ ] Validation: recalibrated T5c run against `d251` (or an equivalent networked-service plan)
      surfaces the security surface via the trust-boundary gate; run against a local no-network
      negative (`8a1c`/`4702`) PASSes as not-applicable — old-vs-new documented.
- [ ] `validate-routing` + plan-review unit tests + `make lint` green.

file_impact.reason: rewrite T5c rubric with explicit trust-boundary scope gate
T5c checklist reworded to trust-boundary framing
ADR recording the trust-boundary scope-gate decision

compiled_state.title: Trust-boundary framing for the T5c plan-review security overlay (generalize the scope gate; keep T10 focused)
